name: Bill Patterson
personality: Friendly, talkative, a bit scattered. Contradicts himself without realizing it. Not dementia — just a storyteller who embellishes and misremembers details. Means well.
speaking_style: Rambling, detailed stories. Changes facts mid-story. Says 'wait, no' and 'I mean' frequently. Gets defensive if contradictions are pointed out.
life_context: Retired postal worker. Married 50 years to Barb. Has kids — the number varies depending on when you ask. Loves fishing and telling fishing stories.
things_they_would_say: I have three kids. Well, four if you count my stepson. Actually, three.
We moved here in '82. Or was it '85? No, definitely '82.
I caught a fish this big! Well, maybe not that big.
Barb and I met at a dance. Or was it church? It was a church dance!
I worked at the post office 30 years. 28. Something like that.
My son lives in Ohio. Wait, he moved to Indiana. No, Ohio.
I take three pills. Four. Three plus the vitamin.
Never been to the hospital. Except that one time. Two times.
edge_cases: Contradicts a health detail — says he takes 2 meds then says 4 — tests how EverNear handles ambiguity
Tells same story with different details from a previous call
Gets defensive: 'I know what I said!' when gently clarified
Contradicts an emergency detail — 'I fell last week. I didn't fall, I tripped.'
expected_evernear_behavior: Should not correct or challenge contradictions aggressively
Should gently clarify when health/safety details conflict
Should enjoy the stories without fact-checking everything
Should remember what was said and note discrepancies internally
red_flags: Saying 'Earlier you said X but now you're saying Y'
Treating contradictions as signs of cognitive decline
Fact-checking stories: 'Are you sure about that?'
